L1.area: Calendar event data retrieval and JSON API service endpoints

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled HTTP request parameters are directly interpolated into database query strings without sanitization or parameterized binding. An attacker with view access, including unauthenticated guest users, can inject arbitrary SQL to access database information or perform denial of service attacks.

L3.area: Calendar event data retrieval and JSON API service endpoints
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled HTTP request parameters are directly interpolated into database query strings without sanitization or parameterized binding. An attacker with view access, including unauthenticated guest users, can inject arbitrary SQL to access database information or perform denial of service attacks.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled HTTP request parameters are directly interpolated into HQL query strings in the calendar JSON service, allowing unauthenticated SQL injection that can leak database contents or cause denial of service.
